description: Renders a parametric curve defined by x(t) and y(t) component expressions over a parameter interval. Suited for spirals, cycloids, Lissajous figures, and other curves not expressible as y = f(x).
utility: Plot a parametric curve x(t), y(t) on a 2D graph

arguments.name: graph
arguments.description: The 2D graph container to plot on

arguments.name: xExpr
arguments.description: x-component as ASCII math string using variable t - e.g., "cos(t)"

arguments.name: yExpr
arguments.description: y-component as ASCII math string using variable t - e.g., "sin(t)"

arguments.name: range
arguments.description: Parameter interval - e.g., range(0, 2*pi). Defaults to 0 to 2*pi

alternates.description: Plot a parametric curve over the given t interval. Both expressions use t as the parameter
Parametric curve with custom stroke color

alternates.syntax:
xf(t) = cos(t)
yf(t) = sin(t)
paraplot(G, xf, yf, range(0, 2*pi))

examples.expression:
G3 = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting))
paraplot(G3, "4cos(7*t)*cos(t)", "4cos(7*t)*sin(t)", range(0, 2*pi), c(green))

G = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-2, 2), range(-2, 2), type(plotting))
paraplot(G, "cos(t)", "sin(t)", range(0, 2*pi))

examples.scenario: Ellipse with parameters a and b
examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-4, 4), range(-3, 3), type(plotting))
a = 3
b = 2
paraplot(G, "a*cos(t)", "b*sin(t)", range(0, 2*pi), c(green))

G = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(0, 14), range(-1, 3), type(plotting))
paraplot(G, "t - sin(t)", "1 - cos(t)", range(0, 4*pi))

G = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-20, 20), range(-20, 20), type(plotting))
paraplot(G, "t*cos(t)", "t*sin(t)", range(0, 6*pi))
